Forecast Assumptions & Seasonal Risk Fuel-related demand shift · Forecast uses present live feeder loading and seasonal PTR history
Operating basis used: 11 kV feeders are checked against a 120 A operational ceiling. Forecast loading is computed from the present recorded feeder load with an +8% summer stress factor. PTR forecast compares the previous summer peak and the March 2026 recorded load, then applies the same summer stress factor to the higher of the two. This forecast should be read as a planning layer for increased electricity consumption under prevailing fuel issues.
Forecast Dashboard Auto-generated from existing dashboard data
Feeder Forecast, Transfer Paths & Remedies Current status and forecast risk are shown separately; transfer paths are based on spare margin feeders
Feeder PTR Type Current Load (A) Current Status Peak Basis (A) 120 A Ceiling Forecast Summer 2026 (A) Forecast Risk Suggested Transfer Path Forecast Remedy / Field Action
Transfer paths are rule-based suggestions using currently low-loaded feeders in the dashboard. Actual execution shall be finalized only after network feasibility, voltage profile, protection coordination and field switching confirmation.
PTR Seasonal Forecast & Remedies Includes previous summer trend to guide preventive steps
PTR Previous Summer Peak (A) Previous Summer % Mar 2026 Recorded (A) Mar 2026 % Forecast Summer 2026 from Peak Basis (A) Forecast % Trend Forecast Remedy / Preventive Action
